channel.title: Comments for page "I just found me a brand new box of matches"
channel.description: Posts in the discussion thread "I just found me a brand new box of matches"

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Help I can't press the rerate button.

Outstanding visualizations and really, your work is always reminiscent of DnD campaigns or some well-bred traveler spinning yarns over a campfire.

I adore it. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Very much enjoyed it. Loved the imagery in all of it, and the characterization for both Lottie and Minzheng made them sound like interesting people, and I certainly want to read more about them. Didn't like the voice for the Sarkite as much, he revealed his plan so quickly it made him feel artificial, unlike the two protagonists.

Anyway, +1 for imagery and characterization of the two leads, and definitely eager to read the next part.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Thanks to Rounderhouse for early crits, and DrChandra and DrLycus for crits on the completed draft.

The idea for this struck me in the comments thread for SCP-4910. Shaggydredlocks seemed to like my very vague and general six-word elevator pitch, so a draft was born.

I'd initially thought it would take place a bit further south, but then I saw KindlyTurtleRock had syncretistic Mekhkanist Mormons heading out to Utah. Lottie was a minor Mekhanist character from my Herman Fuller backstory series whom I was just dying to give a story of her own, so I figured she'd be a great person to take on the grinners.

Slight spoiler / teaser for the next chapter.
Hide small brainstormy teaser.

Almost at the same time, Paychayachachic made a wonderful update to the Church of the Broken God hub, about the history and development of Mekhanism in ancient China. Which made it occur to me that quite a lot of Chinese-American immigrants arrived during this time period in real life history. Thanks to some brainstorming help from Pachayachachic, I determined that in Aces & Eights, at least some of these immigrants might have been Mekhanists interested in a place where they could practice their faith openly (as well as fleeing political upheaval at home).

The series is outlined, and I'll get started on the next chapter as soon as may be. I plan to drag in as many more mellifluous scraps of lore as I can cram into the plot.

Hope you enjoy! :D
